title: arnica angustifolia subsp. attenuata
intro: Arnica angustifolia subsp. attenuata, also known as mountain arnica, is a perennial plant species native to the high-altitude meadows of the European Alps. It is a subspecies of the arnica angustifolia plant, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including bruising, swelling, and pain, making it a valuable remedy for injuries and sports injuries. Arnica angustifolia subsp. attenuata can be found in various herbal preparations, such as topical creams, ointments, and tinctures, which can be applied directly to the affected area for relief.
